questions.question: Describe the difference between a cost-benefit analysis and a cost-effectiveness analysis. Provide an example to illustrate your answer. (5 marks)
questions.hint: Cost-benefit analysis considers both costs and benefits, while cost-effectiveness analysis focuses on the ratio of benefits to costs.

questions.question: What is the purpose of risk evaluation in project management? (5 marks)
questions.hint: Identify the importance of identifying and mitigating risks in project planning and execution.

questions.question: Explain the steps involved in conducting a cost-benefit analysis using the money needed method. (10 marks)
questions.hint: Provide a detailed explanation of the money needed method, including the identification of costs and benefits, calculation of present values, and comparison of results.
